project.overview: Single-coat paint-and-sealer refinish on an existing single-level pressure-treated wood deck. The job covers approximately 416 sq ft of deck floor, 64 linear feet of railing system (top rail, bottom rail, balusters, and posts), and a 6-step staircase (treads and risers). The deck is currently in fair condition. Work includes full surface preparation (power wash, sand rough spots, set nails, mask), application of a single coat of premium paint-and-sealer combination product in slate gray, and complete job-site protection and cleanup. The combined paint-and-sealer system delivers color, waterproofing, and UV protection in one product.
scope: Move and protect homeowner furniture, grill, and personal items
Cover and protect plants, landscaping, and adjacent siding
Power-wash the entire deck surface, stairs, and railing system
Light sanding of rough spots on floor boards, stair treads, and railing surfaces
Set any raised or popped fasteners flush with the deck surface
Apply one coat of premium paint-and-sealer combination product in slate gray
Coat all visible wood surfaces: deck floor, all 6 stair treads, all 6 stair risers, top rails, bottom rails, balusters, and posts
Final touch-ups, complete cleanup, and haul-off of all debris
project.scope_note: Work covers all visible wood surfaces above the deck floor and on the staircase. Underside of deck framing, joists, support posts below the deck floor, and any structural members hidden from normal view are not included. This is a single-coat application; heavily weathered boards may show some grain shadow through the finish. A second coat for full uniform coverage is available as an add-on after Coat 1 is reviewed.

line_items: Surface preparation: power wash, sand rough spots, set fasteners, mask landscaping and siding
Premium materials (paint-and-sealer combination product, brushes, rollers, supplies)
Application of one coat to floor, 6 stairs, and full railing system in slate gray
included: All materials: premium paint-and-sealer product, sandpaper, brushes, pads, rollers, drop cloths, masking
All labor: approximately two working days (Day 1: preparation; Day 2: application)
Full job-site protection of furniture, plants, and siding
1-year limited warranty against premature finish failure caused by defects in workmanship (excludes acts of nature, normal wear, and pre-existing structural conditions)
not_included: Second coat of paint-and-sealer for full uniform coverage (available as an add-on at $1,100 after Coat 1 review)
Repair or replacement of damaged or rotted boards (will be quoted separately if discovered during preparation)
Hardware replacement (screws, nails, joist hangers, fasteners) beyond setting existing raised fasteners
Removal of carpet, outdoor mats, planters, or built-in features
Refinishing of pergolas, gazebos, or attached fences unless explicitly added to scope in writing
Site power supply (homeowner provides access to a working exterior 120V outlet)

schedule.start: Early June 2026 (week of June 1), weather permitting
schedule.duration: 2 working days (Day 1: surface preparation and masking; Day 2: single-coat application and cleanup).
schedule.weather: Application will be rescheduled if rain forecast exceeds 40% during the cure window. A minimum ambient temperature of 50°F day and night is required for proper finish cure.

payment.methods: cash, check, credit or debit card, or digital payment (details provided upon acceptance)
